philosophy: Iterate on small models (Gemma 3 4B + QLoRA r=16, 4-bit) instead of betting compute on a single huge run. Each run below was a deliberate iteration testing one hypothesis about the env or reward shape. The eval set (45 patients) is the source of truth, not the training-time reward curves.

runs.purpose: First end-to-end smoke test of the GRPO loop on a 5-step run.
runs.outcome: Crashed at startup before logging anything. Iteration fix: verified the smoke-test log_prob path before spending compute on real rollouts. No history rows.

runs.purpose: Sanity-check the full curriculum (L1 → L2 → L3) compresses correctly into 10 steps and that each rubric is moving.
runs.outcome: Format = 1.0 throughout, accuracy 25%, total reward mean 1.26. Confirmed the loop trains; tiny step budget too small for real signal. Iteration fix: extend to 50 steps with bigger group size.

runs.purpose: Production run — full action menu in the prompt, 50 steps, group size 6. The adapter promoted to v3 on the Hub.
runs.outcome: Format pinned at 1.0, accuracy 50%, total reward mean 1.63 with healthy variance across rollouts. Action mix stays diverse (5 unique diagnoses, 12-15 differential updates per batch). This is the run with eval_grpo.json on the held-out set.

runs.purpose: Ablation — drop the 2000-token full action menu, keep only a compact valid-IDs list. Hypothesis: 4× cheaper rollouts at no quality cost. Group size bumped to 8 because each rollout is cheaper.
runs.outcome: Faster per step but at step 45 hit unique_diagnoses=1 / diagnosis_mode_rate=1.0 / advantage_std=0.013 — classic mode-collapse signature on a single-disease batch. Reward looks great (~2.9) but only because every rollout converged on the same answer for the same patient. Iteration fix: this is why we kept the full-menu run as the production checkpoint and didn't promote this one. The training-script's own batch monitor flags this with a 'diagnosis mode collapse' warning.

notes: Logs were pulled via the W&B MCP server. To refresh, set WANDB_API_KEY and run `python scripts/build_training_plots.py --refresh`. Without --refresh, the cached JSONLs in this directory are the source of truth.
